title: buildah bud removes existing images from the registry
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**
buildah bud sometimes removes images that were referenced in a build, even when they were already present in the local registry before the build occurred.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Create a Dockerfile for the first stage of the build, and build it using buildah bud.
```
$ cat Dockerfile.image_removal_bug1
FROM alpine
RUN touch /stage1

$ BUILDAH_ISOLATION=rootless buildah bud -f Dockerfile.image_removal_bug1 -t stage1 .
STEP 1: FROM alpine
STEP 2: RUN touch /stage1
STEP 3: COMMIT stage1
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 03901b4a2ea8 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 507b3012320c done
Copying config 3b8c8f8123 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
3b8c8f8123055bdbc9d324d457bfa00ac72f57bc906d7b9a8985d88841f75f2d
```
2. Note that the image was successfully added to the registry:
```
$ BUILDAH_ISOLATION=rootless buildah images
REPOSITORY                 TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED         SIZE
localhost/stage1           latest   ebf4cd4d0f67   3 seconds ago   5.85 MB
docker.io/library/alpine   latest   961769676411   7 weeks ago     5.85 MB
```
2. Create a second Dockerfile that references the tag from the first stage in a FROM line.  Build it once.
```
$ cat Dockerfile.image_removal_bug2
FROM stage1 as stage2.1
FROM alpine as stage2.2
COPY --from=stage2.1 /stage1 /stage2

$ BUILDAH_ISOLATION=rootless buildah bud -f Dockerfile.image_removal_bug2 -t stage2 .
STEP 1: FROM stage1 AS stage2.1
ebf4cd4d0f673260fc9b0c0701fb61fd3d5032afe0877d624d10bf938a01ad84
STEP 2: FROM alpine AS stage2.2
STEP 3: COPY --from=stage2.1 /stage1 /stage2
STEP 4: COMMIT stage2
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 03901b4a2ea8 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 719d842e52c2 done
Copying config 06d963c4ce done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
06d963c4ce895dfa85ab896f02416230a77b52380d40854b0f41b8da1ad6c0e9
```
4. Notice that stage1 is no longer in the registry.
```
$ BUILDAH_ISOLATION=rootless buildah images
REPOSITORY                 TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED         SIZE
localhost/stage2           latest   06d963c4ce89   3 seconds ago   5.85 MB
docker.io/library/alpine   latest   961769676411   7 weeks ago     5.85 MB
```
5. Further note, that this only seems to occur when a tag is referenced in a FROM line, in a stage with no real modifications to the image.  As a counter-example, the following two Dockerfiles do not cause stage1 to be removed:
```
$ cat Dockerfile.image_removal_bug-workaround1
FROM alpine as stage2
COPY --from=stage1 /stage1 /stage2

$ cat Dockerfile.image_removal_bug-workaround2
FROM stage1 as stage2.1
RUN touch /stage2.1
FROM alpine as stage2.2
COPY --from=stage2.1 /stage1 /stage2
```

**Describe the results you received:**
The build for stage2 was successful, but removed the tag from stage1, causing subsequent builds that referenced stage1 to fail.

**Describe the results you expected:**
As with docker build, I expected the build for stage2 to complete, and the image/tag from stage1 to remain in the registry.

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.12.0-dev
Go Version:      go1.10.4
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.1-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  v0.7.1
Git Commit:      13330a45
Built:           Thu Oct 10 13:04:00 2019
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=18.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=bionic
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 18.04.3 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="18.04.3 LTS (Bionic Beaver)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 18.04.3 LTS"
VERSION_ID="18.04"
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
VERSION_CODENAME=bionic
UBUNTU_CODENAME=bionic
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux 01274e57e7a3 4.4.0-151-generic #178-Ubuntu SMP Tue Jun 11 08:30:22 UTC 2019 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**
I suspect this might not be relevant, since running with BUILDAH_ISOLATION=rootless, and with vfs.  But for completeness:
```
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary read-write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional read-only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers (currently overlay, zfs, vfs, btrfs)
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"
```

comments.body: Well, that's an interesting issue.  @bbodiya-akamai thanks for reporting it with the details.  We'll take a look.
This looks like the build is treating the preexisting image as an intermediary image which is reaped during the post-build cleanup. I have a working fix but want to clean up the implementation.

Would like to add that I've hit this as well. I have very simple reproduction example:

```
+   $ ./repro
+ podman pull busybox
Trying to pull docker.io/library/busybox...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 91f30d776fb2 done
Copying config c7c37e472d done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
c7c37e472d31c1685b48f7004fd6a64361c95965587a951692c5f298c6685998
+ buildah images
REPOSITORY                  TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED      SIZE
docker.io/library/busybox   latest   c7c37e472d31   5 days ago   1.45 MB
+ buildah bud -f Dockerfile.a -t example.org/a .
STEP 1: FROM busybox
STEP 2: RUN touch /foo
STEP 3: COMMIT example.org/a
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 50761fe126b6 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 270a45dee6d8 done
Copying config d4a3500ac9 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> d4a3500ac95
d4a3500ac955aa52ee460f4e3d478996bf19a4927aac353c8ab2a91ac3c1709f
+ buildah images
REPOSITORY                  TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED                  SIZE
example.org/a               latest   d4a3500ac955   Less than a second ago   1.45 MB
docker.io/library/busybox   latest   c7c37e472d31   5 days ago               1.45 MB
+ buildah bud -f Dockerfile.b -t example.org/b .
STEP 1: FROM example.org/a
--> d4a3500ac95
STEP 2: FROM scratch
STEP 3: COMMIT example.org/b
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 5f70bf18a086 done
Copying config 0fde735d95 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 0fde735d95c
0fde735d95c31ef322347cfadd1eebf312454125c3c85d06afd43341ebc91ff9
+ buildah images
REPOSITORY                  TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED                  SIZE
example.org/b               latest   0fde735d95c3   Less than a second ago   2.06 KB
docker.io/library/busybox   latest   c7c37e472d31   5 days ago               1.45 MB

+   $ cat repro
#!/bin/sh
set -eux

podman pull busybox

buildah images
buildah bud -f Dockerfile.a -t example.org/a .
buildah images
buildah bud -f Dockerfile.b -t example.org/b .
buildah images

$ cat Dockerfile.a
FROM busybox

RUN touch /foo

$ cat Dockerfile.b
FROM example.org/a

FROM scratch
```

In my use case there seems to be trivial work around, and that is changing `Dockerfile.b` into

```
+   $ cat Dockerfile.b
FROM example.org/a

RUN touch /foo

FROM scratch
```

that leads to expected

```
+   $ buildah images
REPOSITORY                  TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED          SIZE
example.org/b               latest   117ab4e1f885   43 seconds ago   2.06 KB
example.org/a               latest   6bf65cb23d6d   46 seconds ago   1.45 MB
docker.io/library/busybox   latest   c7c37e472d31   5 days ago       1.45 MB
```

@caiges Did you ever finish the fix?

@bbodiya-akamai @graywolf is this still an issue?
@rhatdan I made progress but didn't finish. I can take another stab at it.

> @bbodiya-akamai @graywolf is this still an issue?

Yes.

> @bbodiya-akamai @graywolf is this still an issue?

Yes, I'm still seeing it on 1.19.2
